title: Judge reopens Trump’s IRS suit to examine $1.8bn settlement with justice department
description: Investigation to establish whether ‘anti-weaponization’ fund is ‘product of collusion and itself a fraud’
content: A federal judge has reopened Donald Trump’s $10bn case against the Internal Revenue Service (IRS), after receiving a third-party motion asserting that the settlement, which lacks detail, “is a product of collusion and is itself a fraud on the court”.... [4361 chars]

title: Family pays tribute to girl, 15, who died after swimming at Merseyside beach
description: Chiedza Nyanjowa, who wanted to be a nurse, died in hospital after getting into difficulties in the sea
content:
A 15-year-old girl, who died after getting into difficulties in the sea off the coast of Merseyside, wanted to be a nurse so she could “give back”, her family said in a tribute.
Chiedza Nyanjowa, from Cheshire, was taken to Alder Hey children’s hospi... [3163 chars]

title: Trump proposes ‘wild’ rally instead of concerts after artists back out of series
description: On Saturday posting spree, US president also decries judge’s ruling on Kennedy Center and praises progress on pool
content: In a spree of posts made to his Truth Social account on Saturday, Donald Trump lauded his administration’s efforts to turn the National Mall’s reflecting pool blue, denounced a judge’s ruling removing his name from the Kennedy Center and announced he... [4884 chars]

title: Prostate doctors oppose rationing of cancer screening
description: Survey of urologists in Britain found vast majority want targeted testing for men at high risk
content:
Prostate cancer specialists oppose plans to restrict screening to only a few thousand men, research has shown.
A national survey of urologists in Britain found the vast majority want targeted screening for men at high risk, such as black men and thos... [5892 chars]

title: Billy Gilmour: Scotland midfielder ruled out of World Cup with knee injury
description: Billy Gilmour went off injured in the first half of Scotlands World Cup warm-up win over Curacao at Hampden; Napoli midfielder will return to his club; Scotlands first World Cup game vs Haiti is on June 14
content:
Scotland midfielder Billy Gilmour has been ruled out of the World Cup after suffering a knee injury in the win over Curacao.
The 24-year-old fell to the turf with no one around him in the first half of the 4-1 victory over Dick Advocaat's side at Ham... [2413 chars]
